rationale: Re-ran my round-1 mutation in an isolated /tmp archive of a2f2451 (never touched the shared worktree). Applied `false &&` to leg 9.5's UnresolvedVerdicts() check and re-ran `go test ./internal/panel -run UnresolvedRequestChanges -v`: all three rows now FAIL, including the previously-hollow 'two unresolved RC slots' row, which fails with exactly 'message missing "revA"' / 'message missing "revB"' — confirming revA/revB are NOT fixed substrings of the Block message (they only appear via the %s slot-list substitution) and the row now genuinely discriminates leg 9.5's multi-slot naming. Reverted the mutation (diff against a saved backup was identical) and confirmed all three rows PASS on real code, plus TestPanelGate_RequestChangesBlocksComplete passes and its new 'unresolved REQUEST_CHANGES' assertion matches gate.go's actual leg-9.5 message text verbatim. `git diff 4dfebbcb a2f2451 -- internal/panel/gate.go` is a single-word comment reword ('byte-superset' -> 'substring-set superset'), no logic change. go build/gofmt/vet all clean in the tmp copy. My round-1 finding is ADDRESSED.

findings.location: internal/panel/panel_decision_test.go: 'two unresolved RC slots' subtest (round-1 finding)
findings.description: Round-1: the row's mustHave checks for slot names 'x'/'y' were trivially satisfied by incidental substrings in every Block message ('/ms-bead-fix' contains 'x'; 'bypass'/'only' contain 'y'), so the row passed even with leg 9.5 fully disabled. Round-2 fix: slots renamed to 'revA'/'revB', which appear ONLY via the %s slot-list substitution and are not fixed substrings anywhere in the Block message format. Mutation-verified in an isolated /tmp copy of a2f2451: with leg 9.5 disabled (`false && len(unresolved) > 0`), the row now fails with 'message missing "revA"' and 'message missing "revB"'. With the mutation reverted, the row (and the other two) pass on real code. Fix confirmed genuine.

Round-1 low-severity robustness note: the e2e test's failure under mutation was previously masked by an unrelated doc-sync gate tripping on the same fixture, rather than pinning specifically to leg 9.5. Round-2 fix adds `assert msg contains "unresolved REQUEST_CHANGES"`. Verified this substring appears verbatim in gate.go's leg-9.5 message format (line ~279: "...unresolved REQUEST_CHANGES / non-APPROVE verdict(s) from..."), and confirmed the e2e test still PASSES against real (unmutated) code in the /tmp copy — so the new assertion is sound and genuinely pins the block to leg 9.5.
